title: myriactis westonii
intro: Myriactis westonii is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the western myriactis or sticky geranium, characterized by its sticky leaves and pink or purple flowers. The medicinal properties of Myriactis westonii lie in its ability to exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic activities, making it a valuable component in traditional herbal medicine. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to soothe wounds and reduce inflammation. Myriactis westonii can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, allowing for a range of applications and uses.
